detail: Line 154 checks `v.trim() === value.trim()` but `v` IS the value being submitted, so this condition is always true. The check appears to be intended as defensive logic but adds no filtering. This dead code is at lines 154-157 inside the if block.
fix: Remove the redundant trim comparison or clarify the intent—if checking for exact command match, use the filtered command list directly.

title: Missing fromHistory reset in autocomplete completion
detail: When Enter is pressed and autocomplete completes a command (lines 164-167), the code does not call `setFromHistory(false)`. If the user navigated history, pressed up/down to enter history mode, then pressed Enter on an autocomplete suggestion, fromHistory remains true, potentially breaking subsequent ↑↓ history navigation.
fix: Add `setFromHistory(false)` before `onChange(cmd.name + " ")` at line 164.

title: Inconsistent overlay positioning between flow and fullscreen modes
detail: In flow mode, overlay is rendered inside the scrollable box (line 119: `{!fullscreen && overlay}`), but in fullscreen mode, it's rendered as a separate flex item outside scrollable (lines 134-138). This means the overlay has different positioning semantics and may have different z-order or scroll behavior depending on mode, potentially causing layout bugs or unexpected behavior during mode transitions.
fix: Move overlay rendering logic into a consistent location—either always as a separate flex item after scrollable (checking fullscreen in the content check), or always inside scrollable (update fullscreen logic).

title: Duplicated utility functions fmtTokens and modelTags
detail: The functions fmtTokens (lines 411-416) and modelTags (lines 419-428) are identical to those in ModelSelector.tsx (lines 21-27, 30-39). This code duplication makes maintenance harder when these utilities need updates.
fix: Extract fmtTokens and modelTags to a shared utilities file (e.g., packages/tui/src/ui/utils/modelFormatting.ts) and import them in both ModelManager.tsx and ModelSelector.tsx.

title: Duplicated utility functions fmtTokens and modelTags
detail: The functions fmtTokens (lines 21-27) and modelTags (lines 30-39) are identical to those in ModelManager.tsx (lines 411-416, 419-428). This code duplication makes maintenance harder when these utilities need updates.
fix: Extract fmtTokens and modelTags to a shared utilities file (e.g., packages/tui/src/ui/utils/modelFormatting.ts) and import them in both ModelManager.tsx and ModelSelector.tsx.

title: Resource leak on unhandled rejection from waitUntilExit()
detail: If instance.waitUntilExit() rejects (or throws), the keepAlive interval is not cleared and flushHistorySync()/instance.cleanup() do not execute. The code assumes waitUntilExit() never rejects, but there is no try-catch to handle errors. This leaves the interval running and history unflushed on error.
fix: Wrap the await in try-catch-finally: try { await instance.waitUntilExit(); } finally { clearInterval(keepAlive); flushHistorySync(); instance.cleanup(); }

title: Race condition: instance assigned asynchronously but used synchronously
detail: instance is assigned inside the .then() callback at line 46, but finish() (called by OnboardingPrompt callbacks) tries to call instance.unmount() and instance.cleanup() at lines 26-27. If the component completes before render() settles, instance is still undefined, causing a runtime error accessing properties on undefined.
fix: Await the render promise before subscribing to the component callbacks, or guard the finish() function with a check like 'if (!instance) return'.

title: getEntries() returns internal array reference without copying
detail: The getEntries() method at line 75 returns this.entries directly without creating a copy. This allows callers to mutate the store's internal state by modifying the returned array, bypassing the notify() mechanism and potentially causing the store to become inconsistent with subscribers' views.
fix: Return [...this.entries] or this.entries.slice() to prevent external mutations.

title: notify() lacks error isolation for listener failures
detail: The notify() loop at lines 151-153 does not wrap listener calls in try-catch. If any listener throws an error, subsequent listeners in the Set will not be called, leaving the UI out of sync. QueryGuard.ts (lines 33-41) demonstrates the correct pattern with defensive error isolation.
fix: Wrap listener calls in try-catch blocks to ensure all listeners fire regardless of individual failures, matching the error handling pattern in QueryGuard.

title: Resize event listener never unregistered
detail: The resize listener registered at line 50 via stdout.on('resize', ...) is never removed. If TerminalRenderer instances are created and destroyed repeatedly, the listeners accumulate on the stdout stream, causing memory leaks and potential duplicate event handling.
fix: Store the listener and unregister it in a cleanup/destroy method, or use once() if only a single resize needs handling. Alternatively, manage listener lifecycle explicitly in exit().

title: Newline truncation always appends ellipsis even when no truncation occurs
detail: When singleLine=true and a newline is found at line 143-145, the code extracts the prefix. Line 147 checks if the prefix width plus 1 exceeds maxWidth. If it does NOT exceed (condition is false), line 150 unconditionally appends '…' via return `${result}…`. This adds an ellipsis even though the result fits within maxWidth, violating the pattern that ellipsis indicates actual truncation. The condition should only append ellipsis when truncateToWidth() is called (line 148), not unconditionally.
fix: Change line 150 to return `result` without appending '…', since the prefix already fits and no truncation occurred at the newline boundary.

title: Cursor position not re-clamped after deletion in 'x' command
detail: The 'x' command (line 127-131) deletes the character at cursor position, but does not adjust the cursor afterward. If cursor was at the end of text before deletion, it remains there but now points past the end of the modified text. For example, if text='ab' and cursor=1 (at 'b'), after deletion text='a' but cursor=1 (out of bounds).
fix: After line 129, add: s.cursor = Math.min(s.cursor, text.length > 0 ? text.length - 1 : 0);

title: Confusing env var semantics: CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1 disables fullscreen but function returns true
detail: The function isFullscreenEnvEnabled() uses CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER, where '1' (NO_FLICKER set truthy) causes the function to return true (fullscreen enabled). This is semantically backwards: setting 'NO_FLICKER=1' should disable fullscreen (return false), not enable it. Line 77 returns true when isEnvTruthy(CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER), and line 76 returns false when isEnvDefinedFalsy. The logic inverts the variable's semantic intent.
fix: Invert the return values: `if (isEnvDefinedFalsy(...)) return true` (flicker disabled by default) and `if (isEnvTruthy(...)) return false` (NO_FLICKER=1 should disable fullscreen). Or rename the variable to CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_FULLSCREEN for clarity.

title: Shell redirections in which checks fail without shell: true
detail: The execSync calls on lines 90 and 94 use shell OR operators (||) and redirections (2>/dev/null) which require shell interpretation, but do not pass { shell: true } option. The commands will fail because || is treated as a literal argument.
fix: Add { shell: true } option to both execSync calls in isRecordingAvailable()
